metadata.name: Caddy
metadata.description: Modern web server and reverse proxy with automatic HTTPS, modular Caddyfile config, and simple presets for static sites or upstream proxying.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Caddy can serve static sites and reverse proxy upstream applications from the same modular configuration layout.

## Prerequisites
- Decide whether this instance is serving a static site, reverse proxying to another container, or both.
- Use real public domain names if you want publicly trusted certificates from Let's Encrypt.
- Choose the site content storage mode up front if you plan to serve static files.
- Avoid assigning the same site address in multiple generated site blocks unless you intend to merge that logic manually.
- Expose ports 80 and 443 if you expect public ACME certificate issuance to work.

## Deploy
- Render the template to create `compose.yaml`, `Caddyfile`, and the generated `config/webserver.caddy` site config.
- Review the generated config, especially any enabled webserver or reverse proxy blocks and any optional custom Caddyfile content.
- Start the stack with `docker compose up -d`.
- Check the first startup with `docker compose logs -f` and confirm certificate issuance or upstream connectivity.

## Post-Install
- Put static site content in the configured site directory or NFS export when the webserver preset is enabled.
- Add or edit `config/*.caddy` files when you need behavior beyond the built-in presets.
- Keep the named Caddy data volumes so certificate state and runtime data survive container recreation.

## Notes
- Automatic HTTPS is enabled by Caddy based on each site address. Use real hostnames in the generated webserver or reverse proxy blocks if you want public certificates.
- The TLS section only adds optional global ACME email metadata for the stock Caddy image. DNS challenge is intentionally not part of this template.
- The webserver preset always serves from `/srv` inside the container and requires site content storage to be configured when enabled.
- Optional custom config fields are appended only when they contain non-empty content.

## References
- [Caddy](https://caddyserver.com/)
- [Documentation](https://caddyserver.com/docs/)
- [Automatic HTTPS](https://caddyserver.com/docs/automatic-https)
- [Global Options](https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/options)
- [reverse_proxy Directive](https://caddyserver.com/docs/caddyfile/directives/reverse_proxy)
- [Docker Image](https://hub.docker.com/_/caddy)

variables.title: General
variables.description: Basic container naming and lifecycle settings.

variables.name: service_name
variables.title: Service Name
variables.description: Compose service name for the Caddy container.

variables.name: restart_policy
variables.title: Restart Policy
variables.description: Restart policy applied to the container.

variables.name: container_timezone
variables.title: Container Timezone
variables.description: Optional timezone passed through as the `TZ` environment variable.

variables.name: general

variables.title: Network
variables.description: Port publishing and Docker network attachment.

variables.name: ports_http
variables.title: HTTP Port
variables.description: Host port published for HTTP traffic on container port 80.

variables.name: ports_https
variables.title: HTTPS Port
variables.description: Host port published for HTTPS traffic on container port 443.

variables.name: docker_network
variables.title: Docker Network
variables.description: Docker network that Caddy should join.

variables.name: docker_network_external
variables.title: External Network
variables.description: Use an existing external Docker network instead of creating one.

variables.name: network

variables.title: TLS
variables.description: Optional global ACME metadata for Caddy.

variables.name: tls_enabled
variables.title: TLS Settings
variables.description: Enable optional global ACME metadata in the main `Caddyfile`.

variables.name: tls_email
variables.title: ACME Email
variables.description: Email address used for ACME account registration.

variables.name: tls

variables.title: Webserver
variables.description: Optional generated static site config that serves files from `/srv`.

variables.name: webserver_enabled
variables.title: Static Webserver
variables.description: Render a basic static site config into `config/webserver.caddy`.

variables.name: webserver_domain
variables.title: Site Address
variables.description: Hostname or site address used by the generated static site config.

variables.name: webserver_content_type
variables.title: Site Content
variables.description: Storage mode used for static site content mounted into `/srv`.

variables.name: webserver_content_mount_path
variables.title: Bind Mount Path
variables.description: Host path used when site content is mounted directly from the filesystem.

variables.name: webserver_content_nfs_server
variables.title: NFS Server
variables.description: Hostname or IP address of the NFS server that stores site content.

variables.name: webserver_content_nfs_path
variables.title: NFS Export Path
variables.description: Export path on the NFS server for site content.

variables.name: webserver_content_nfs_options
variables.title: NFS Mount Options
variables.description: Mount options used for the NFS-backed site content volume.

variables.name: webserver_custom_config_content
variables.title: Custom Config Content
variables.description: Optional additional Caddyfile directives inserted into the generated webserver site block when not empty.

variables.name: webserver

variables.title: Reverse Proxy
variables.description: Optional generated reverse proxy config for forwarding requests to an upstream service.

variables.name: reverse_proxy_enabled
variables.title: Reverse Proxy
variables.description: Append a reverse proxy site block to `config/webserver.caddy`.

variables.name: reverse_proxy_domain
variables.title: Site Address
variables.description: Hostname or site address used by the generated reverse proxy config.

variables.name: reverse_proxy_upstream
variables.title: Upstream
variables.description: Upstream target used by the generated reverse proxy config, for example `app:3000`.

variables.name: reverse_proxy_custom_config_content
variables.title: Custom Config Content
variables.description: Optional additional `reverse_proxy` subdirectives inserted into the generated reverse proxy block when not empty.

variables.name: reverse-proxy
